opp.next_step: 3/16 BCE: Awaiting RFP 12/26 BCE: Awaiting RFP. Spoke with Brian, still undergoing RFP creation 10/24 BCE: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos. Making a list of requirements and a RFP. Should hear back end of October for RFP 9/29 BCE: Reaching out 9/19 BCE: Awaiting response. will reach out next EOW 9/12 BCE: Showed demo yesterday. Received well. The team is meeting internally to shortlist, should hear back soon 8/28 Meeting scheduled to restart project 8/15 BCE: Reaching out once more next week. If nothing, closing out. 7/14 Likely closeout, they have gone dark 6/30 Cory was recpetive on the intro call but has been quiet again. We need to decide if this is worth keeping in pipeline 6/9 BCE: Intro occurred. Looking at P2P, with S2P on wishlist. Currently using DDSI but exploring other options. Timeline would be 2-3 years out for P2P replacement. Sending follow up email to get approx. user count for BP price quote. 6/2/25 BCE: Scheduled reconnect intro with Cory and Elissa on 6/9 to align on where we are and next steps --------------------- LCS Opportunity Summary – Christian Q1–Q2 2025 Working with Cory Griffiths (Director of Procurement) and Elissa Rogers to schedule a high-level overview for executive leadership. Invite sent for 6/4 meeting. Cory confirmed ongoing internal discussions; next steps include executive alignment, high-level overview, and subsequent demo. Christian initially met with Cory on 4/2. LCS (including PCS subsidiary) expressed need for a full-suite S2P platform, preferring one provider over multiple point solutions. Areas of interest: Procurement, Sourcing, Spend Analytics, Contracts, Supplier Management, Project & Savings Management. Budget confirmed (~$150K+ SaaS, equal or more for Services); no RFP expected. Zycus references with Brookdale and Benchmark (peers in senior living) resonated. Q1 2025 Rod Copple (CFO) handed procurement ownership to Cory. Outreach began in February after his onboarding. Multiple touchpoints with Rod and Christian to schedule initial calls and align stakeholders. Procurement Pinnacle invite extended. Email and call follow-ups sent in March and April to drive meeting scheduling and share materials via Showpad. Late 2024 Opportunity stalled with Rod; strategy shifted to target executive team (CFO, CPO) using peer success stories (Brookdale, Benchmark). Rod was actively engaging with shared content but unresponsive to meeting invites. Intent observed on Showpad. RSD attempted re-engagement via direct calls, content sharing, and high-value metrics. Secure content site created for LCS review.
opp.ais_why:
• Champion Cory Griffiths engaged directly with the Autonomous Supplier Agent and Autonomous Negotiation Agent on the September 11 2025 demo, and asked specifically about Teams conversational AI integration. Initial champion Rod Copple framed the relationship around Merlin solving manual process pain. The Merlin, Agentic AI and ANA product line is in scope, with AI importance recorded as Important.
• The 84-minute LCS demo carried 13 senior attendees, with the buyer-driven closing action item being internal conversations to define specific use cases and business requirements before deeper sessions. The Avoma notes for this call are still pending generation, so direct transcript verbatim is not yet available, but the rep-captured agentic-platform notes and Gen AI commentary independently anchor the AI-led posture.
• Verbatim previously captured at action-item level; full transcript review was not possible because Avoma notes for this meeting are not yet ready. The Hungry call rests on the rep-logged champion AI engagement plus a multi-attendee senior demo with explicit AI follow-through. Confidence is moderated to Medium pending transcript-level corroboration.

contacts.name: Rod Copple
contacts.title: Vice President, Director of Integration Services

items.quote: Budget confirmed (~$150K+ SaaS, equal or more for Services); no RFP expected

items.area: No champion secured; single-threaded through an unresponsive contact
items.quote: Cory was receptive on the intro call but has been quiet again.

items.area: Value case unquantified (no ROI / savings figure)
items.quote: The spend analytics is very important

metrics.narrative: LCS articulated a qualitative case, never a quantified one. On the 11 Sep 2025 demo Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO, LCS Group Purchasing) said 'the spend analytics is very important' and stressed that '50% of that for us comes from outside of our organization with our non-LCS related clients', framing a group-purchasing value-partnership need to extend analytics to member communities. The underlying driver is consolidation onto one full-suite S2P provider rather than point solutions (Next Step summary, Q1-Q2 2025). No cost of the problem, savings target, cycle-time or headcount number has ever been captured, so quantifying the value case with LCS finance (Robbie Rushing) is a required move.

champion.narrative: No champion has been secured. Cory Griffiths (Dir-Procurement) was the intended champion but did not attend the 11 Sep 2025 demo, showed no advocacy behaviours, and is now unresponsive (Next Step 2025-06-30: 'Cory was receptive on the intro call but has been quiet again'; last activity 2026-06-15). The strongest developing relationship is Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO), who engaged deeply and asked the most substantive questions on the demo, but she has not been cultivated as a sponsor and has not been contacted since. Robbie Rushing (Sr. Finance Manager) and Elissa Rogers (Procurement Analyst) were engaged on the demo and are the best multi-thread footholds. Building Elisa Baptiste into an executive sponsor and re-establishing 2-3 live threads is prerequisite to any commercial step.

competition.narrative: The real threat is inertia, not a named rival. DDSI is the incumbent, and the buyer set its own P2P replacement horizon at 2-3 years out (Next Step 2025-06-09), so 'do nothing / status quo' is the leading alternative. Salesforce lists Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba and iCertis, and the buyer confirmed in Oct 2025 they were 'still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos', but none has been buyer-preferred and there has been no competitive intel since Sep 2025. Zycus's wedge is senior-living peer proof (Brookdale and Benchmark) plus the Merlin agentic-sourcing and negotiation story, which landed as 'super interesting' with the small procurement team; converting that into a differentiated, quantified proposal is how we displace both the incumbent and the field.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is clear but not urgent. LCS runs DDSI today and is exploring alternatives (Next Step 2025-06-09), and wants a single full-suite S2P provider instead of point solutions (Next Step summary, Q1-Q2 2025), implying fragmentation and integration pain. On the demo a small procurement team saw autonomous sourcing as relief for unmanaged tailspin (Elissa Rogers, Procurement Analyst: 'We're a pretty small team over in procurement, so this would definitely help us'), and Elisa Baptiste framed a group-purchasing need to extend value to non-LCS members. The pain owners are Cory Griffiths (procurement) and Elisa Baptiste (COO). The critical weakness is urgency: the buyer's own 2-3 year replacement horizon and 'a lot more here than we're ready to take on' comment mean there is no compelling event.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have surfaced in any call, task, or field: no MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, or InfoSec process has been discussed, and no contract-expiration forcing function is on record. The deal has never reached pricing or commercials, so paper process is entirely unknown. Visibility would come only from re-engaging the buyer and co-authoring a mutual timeline once a live evaluation resumes.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Rod Copple = Decision Maker; Elisa Baptiste = SVP COO)
economic_buyer.narrative: Budget was verbally confirmed at roughly $150K+ for SaaS and equal-or-more for services (Next Step summary, Q1-Q2 2025), but the person who approves it is not confirmed. The most senior engaged voice is Elisa Baptiste (SVP Chief Operating Officer, LCS Group Purchasing), who personally drove the Q&A on the 11 Sep 2025 demo (spend analytics, ERP and Salesforce integration, data classification), yet she carries no Salesforce economic-buyer role and has not been re-engaged since. Rod Copple (VP, Director of Integration Services) is the Salesforce Decision Maker but handed procurement ownership to Cory Griffiths in Q1 2025 and has since exited the thread. Confirming the budget approver and cultivating Elisa Baptiste as executive sponsor is the priority.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer signalled an RFP-driven, committee-based selection: after the Sep 2025 demo the team was to meet internally to shortlist (Next Step 2025-09-12), and Brian Lambert (Program Manager, IT) owned RFP creation (Next Step 2025-12-26). That process has stalled: the RFP promised for end Oct 2025 was still unwritten at 2026-03-16 and remains undelivered. There is no confirmed approval chain, no decision date, and the buyer's own stated horizon for a P2P replacement was 2-3 years out (Next Step 2025-06-09). The move is to obtain the RFP status and a concrete decision timeline in writing from Brian Lambert.

sources: Avoma demo 2025-09-11 (Elisa Baptiste, Robbie Rushing, Elissa Rogers)
decision_criteria.narrative: The 11 Sep 2025 demo surfaced concrete evaluation interests even though no formal weighted criteria exist yet: sourcing-event flow, spend analytics including externally-supplied member data, eProcurement, and integration with Salesforce and their ERP (Elisa Baptiste: 'Salesforce is a very big tool for us'). Robbie Rushing pressed on importing external member data into analytics, and Elisa Baptiste asked for side-by-side contract clause comparison and probed the data classification/normalization QA process ('one of the things I really wanna understand... a little bit more about the classification normalization'). These are confirmed buyer-stated interests; the formal, prioritized criteria are expected to arrive with the RFP, which has never been delivered.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Formal Evaluation' anchors near 35. Thin signals pull the read to 27.
blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Live next steps with named dates

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Recency-weighted, the strongest current threat is inertia: incumbent DDSI with the buyer's self-stated 2-3 year replacement horizon (2025-06-09) means 'do nothing' leads.

bullets.text: Buyer's own P2P replacement horizon is 2-3 years out and no compelling event exists; the deal has drifted for 22+ months.
The senior-most engaged exec (Elisa Baptiste, SVP COO) has not been re-engaged since Sep 2025 and no economic buyer is confirmed.
Single-threaded through Cory Griffiths, who is unresponsive; no champion advocacy and no multi-thread to power.
Budget verbally confirmed (~$150K+ SaaS) but no pricing or proposal has ever been presented to validate it.

cro_panel.header: LCS (Life Care Services) — Formal Evaluation · $195K · closes 27 Aug

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 27 is carried by momentum 55 vs stage-expected 52; exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 15 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 11 is anchored on the stage win (27), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 15 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 7 / Moderate with AI importance 'Important'. The Sep 2025 demo corroborates real but non-decisive appetite: strong engagement with agentic sourcing and Merlin negotiation, tempered by 'a lot more here than we're ready to take on at the moment', so the field is fairly rated, not over-rated.

items.change: 11 Sep 2025 demo transcript now available, confirming a 13-person senior room led by SVP COO Elisa Baptiste with deep AI and analytics engagement
RFP promised for end Oct 2025 still undelivered and open at last mention
Close date pushed from 17 Jun 2026 to 27 Aug 2026 with no stage progression
June re-engagement attempts to Brian Lambert and Elisa Baptiste returned only an out-of-office auto-reply

deal_movement.summary: Rep-side motion only. The one substantive event, the 11 Sep 2025 multi-attendee demo, now has a full transcript confirming deep COO-level engagement, but nothing has advanced since: the RFP promised for end Oct 2025 never arrived, the close date slipped four times, and the only recent buyer signal is an out-of-office auto-reply to June outreach. The pulse is dark.

champion_strength.source: Avoma demo 2025-09-11; SF Next_Step__c 2025-06-30; SF OpportunityContactRole
champion_strength.summary: No champion is secured. Cory Griffiths, the intended champion, skipped the Sep 2025 demo and is unresponsive. The strongest developing relationship is Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO), who engaged deeply on the demo but has not been cultivated or re-contacted since; Elissa Rogers is a positive but junior potential coach. What has developed is executive-level interest on one call; what is missing is any advocacy, internal driving, or access being extended to Zycus.
champion_strength.champion: None confirmed (Elisa Baptiste is the strongest developing relationship)

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is dark. The last real buyer touch was the Sep 2025 demo; every signal since is rep-side, and the only buyer reply in 2026 was an out-of-office auto-reply. This is cold, not merely cooling.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 28 Aug 2024 (674 days old). Formal Evaluation, close 27 Aug 2026 is 55 days out. Last buyer-facing engagement was the 11 Sep 2025 demo, ~295 days ago; every touch since is rep-side (Clari emails / call attempts 15 Jun 2026) and the only buyer reply was Brian Lambert's out-of-office auto-reply. RFP promised end Oct 2025, still open at 2026-03-16, 8+ months overdue. Close date moved 2025-10-31, 2025-12-08, 2026-02-06 and 2026-03-27 with no stage progression. RFP receipt, pricing, validation, negotiation and contracting in 55 days is not credible.
evidence: Next Step 2025-12-26: Awaiting RFP, spoke with Brian, still undergoing RFP creation
Next Step 2025-10-24: still evaluating other vendors with high-level demos, making a list of requirements and an RFP
Next Step 2025-09-12: Showed demo yesterday, received well, team meeting internally to shortlist
Task 2026-06-15: Clari email to Brian and Elisa; buyer reply was an out-of-office auto-reply
CloseDate slips: 2025-10-31, 2025-12-08, 2026-02-06, 2026-03-27 (all by Bailey Erazo / Michael McCarthy)
Avoma demo 2025-09-11: Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO) closed with 'probably a lot more here than we're ready to take on at the moment'
north_star_verdict.headline: Cold: the last real buyer engagement was the 11 Sep 2025 demo (10 months ago); the RFP promised for end Oct 2025 never arrived, the close date has slipped four times, no economic buyer is engaged, and June outreach drew only an out-of-office auto-reply. Best Case is indefensible.

flags: Cultivate Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO), the demo's most engaged voice, into an executive sponsor before any commercial step.
Re-establish 2-3 live threads (Robbie Rushing finance, Elissa Rogers procurement); the deal is single-threaded through unresponsive Cory Griffiths.
Force a qualification decision: the RFP is 8+ months overdue and June outreach drew only an auto-reply, so secure a concrete timeline or disposition honestly.
best_practice_check.summary: Cold, single-threaded early-stage deal with a strong-but-uncultivated executive contact and a missing RFP; win strategy is re-engagement through the COO, multi-threading, and an honest qualification decision.

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, the strongest current threat is inertia: incumbent DDSI with the buyer's self-stated 2-3 year replacement horizon (2025-06-09) means 'do nothing' leads. Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba and iCertis sit in the Salesforce field and were part of an active 'evaluating other vendors' state in Oct 2025, but none has been buyer-preferred and all are dormant after 10 months of silence with no shortlist outcome. Zycus's edge is senior-living peer proof (Brookdale, Benchmark) and the Merlin agentic story.

competitors.name: DDSI
competitors.quote: Currently using DDSI but exploring other options. Timeline would be 2-3 years out for P2P replacement.
competitors.how_we_win: Displace on full-suite consolidation and senior-living peer proof (Brookdale/Benchmark); use the 2-3 year horizon to inject a compelling event rather than let the incumbent coast.

competitors.name: Status quo / do nothing
competitors.quote: There's probably a lot more here than we're ready to take on at the moment.
competitors.how_we_win: Land a modular, phase-1 scope (P2P or source-to-contract) with a quantified savings case so adoption feels small and urgent.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Merlin native agentic sourcing and no-charge supplier licensing against Coupa's cost profile once a live evaluation resumes.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with faster time-to-value and senior-living references for a small procurement team.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos.
competitors.how_we_win: Position integrated intake-to-pay plus Merlin over point-suite complexity.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast ease-of-adoption and total cost for an organization of LCS's size against SAP complexity.

competitors.name: iCertis
competitors.quote: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos.
competitors.how_we_win: Show CLM as one native module of a full S2P suite rather than a standalone contracting tool.

items.deliverable: LCS to deliver the RFP (and requirements list) Brian Lambert has owned since Sep 2025

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin agentic-sourcing, autonomous negotiation, and contract-discovery story was demonstrated in depth on 11 Sep 2025 and drew genuine interest ('super interesting') from the small procurement team, so AI is well positioned as a differentiator. It is under-converted, not under-positioned: the appetite exists but has not been turned into a scoped, quantified next step.
